params.description: Vertical picture graph appears on left. Scale of 2. Each symbol = 2.
Vertical picture graph appears on right. Scale of 5. Each symbol = 5. Key highlighted.

beats.text: In Warmup, you saw something important. Same data, but the scale of 5 graph needed fewer symbols. That's because each symbol represents MORE.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph on left. Books Read This Week data. Scale of 2.
Vertical picture graph on right. Same data. Scale of 5. Key highlighted. Multiple choice active.

beats.text: Same data, fewer symbols. That's why scale of 5 is useful when numbers get bigger. What does each symbol represent when the scale is 5?
What does each symbol represent when the scale is 5?

params.description: Data table appears. Favorite Playground Activities. Swings: 20, Slides: 15, Monkey Bars: 25, Sandbox: 10. Vertical orientation.

beats.text: Here is a table showing what playground activities students voted for as their favorites. Let's see how 20 items become symbols when the scale is 5. Watch. We'll group by 5s.

params.description: 20 items appear for Swings category. Key visible: Each ⭐ = 5.

elements.description: Data table showing Favorite Playground Activities. Swings: 20, Slides: 15, Monkey Bars: 25, Sandbox: 10.
Vertical picture graph. Swings column shows 4 star symbols stacked. Key: Each ⭐ = 5. Other columns empty.

params.description: 20 items cluster into 4 groups of 5.

beats.text: 5, 10, 15, 20. That's 4 groups of 5.

params.description: 4 groups transform into 4 symbols, stacking upward on picture graph for Swings column.

beats.text: 20 items equals 4 symbols. Each symbol shows 5.

params.description: Vertical picture graph appears. Swings column has 4 star symbols. Other columns empty. Key visible: Each star = 5.
Data table appears alongside picture graph showing same playground data.

beats.text: It's easier to read this picture graph if you can count by 5s. That's exactly what we do with scale of 5.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph in reading mode. Favorite Playground Activities. Swings column: 4 star symbols. Other columns empty. Key: Each ⭐ = 5. multiple_choice tool active.
Data table showing Favorite Playground Activities values alongside the graph.

beats.text: Let's check: does the picture graph for the Swings category match our data table? 4 symbols, scale of 5. Count by 5s. What's the total?
What is the total represented by the picture graph for Swings?

params.description: Vertical data table titled Favorite Playground Activities appears. Categories: Swings, Slides, Monkey Bars, Sandbox. Values: Swings 20, Slides 15, Monkey Bars 25, Sandbox 10.

Vertical picture graph appears in building mode. Favorite Playground Activities. Scale 5 (Each symbol = 5). Swings column has 4 symbols already placed. Slides, Monkey Bars, Sandbox columns empty. Key visible.

beats.text: 15 students said slides are their favorite equipment on the playground. How many symbols is that?

elements.description: Vertical data table showing Favorite Playground Activities data. Swings 20, Slides 15, Monkey Bars 25, Sandbox 10.
Vertical picture graph in building mode. Scale 5. Swings has 4 symbols. Slides has 3 symbols. Monkey Bars and Sandbox empty. click_to_place tool active.

beats.text: Think: count by 5s until you reach 15. How many 5s? Click to set the height of the Slides.
Set the number of symbols for Slides.

params.description: Vertical picture graph appears in building mode. Favorite Playground Equipment data. Swings has 4 symbols (20 votes), Slides has 3 symbols (15 votes). Monkey Bars and Sandbox rows are empty. Key visible: each symbol = 5 votes.
Data table appears alongside graph showing Favorite Playground Equipment values: Swings 20, Slides 15, Monkey Bars 25, Sandbox 10.

beats.text: Keep building the picture graph. Monkey Bars has 25. That's a bigger number. Click to set the height of the Monkey Bars.
Set the height for Monkey Bars.

params.description: Vertical picture graph appears. Favorite Playground Equipment data. Swings column has 4 symbols, Slides column has 3 symbols, Monkey Bars column has 5 symbols, Sandbox column empty. Key: each symbol = 5 votes.
Data table appears alongside graph, showing Favorite Playground Equipment values: Swings=20, Slides=15, Monkey Bars=25, Sandbox=10.

beats.text: Let's complete the picture graph. Sandbox has 10 votes. That's the smallest number.
Set the height for Sandbox.

params.description: Vertical picture graph appears. Favorite Playground Activities data. Swings=4 symbols, Slides=3 symbols, Monkey Bars=5 symbols, Sandbox=2 symbols. Key visible: each star symbol = 5 votes.

beats.text: You made a scaled picture graph. Let's read it. Which activity is the most popular?
Click on the activity that is most popular.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph in reading mode. Favorite Playground Activities. Sandbox column highlighted. Scale 5. Swings 4 symbols, Slides 3 symbols, Monkey Bars 5 symbols, Sandbox 2 symbols.

params.description: Previous highlight clears.

beats.text: And which got the fewest?
Click on the category with the fewest.

params.description: Vertical picture graph appears on left side. Favorite Playground Activities data. Swings 4 symbols, Slides 3 symbols, Monkey Bars 5 symbols, Sandbox 2 symbols. Scale 5: each symbol = 5 votes. Key visible.

beats.text: You made a picture graph with scale of 5. Now let's see another way to show the exact same data.

params.description: Vertical picture graph appears. Favorite Playground Activities data. Swings=4 symbols, Slides=3, Monkey Bars=5, Sandbox=2. Key visible: Each ⭐ = 5.
Data table appears showing Playground Activities values: Swings=20, Slides=15, Monkey Bars=25, Sandbox=10.

beats.text: Here's the same Playground data from the picture graph as a bar graph. You've read bar graphs before.

params.description: Picture graph transforms into vertical bar graph. Categories: Swings=20, Slides=15, Monkey Bars=25, Sandbox=10. Axis labeled 0–30 in 5s. All 4 bars pre-filled.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph in reading mode. Favorite Playground Activities. Swings 4 symbols, Slides 3, Monkey Bars 5, Sandbox 2. Key: Each star = 5.
Vertical bar graph showing same data. Swings 20, Slides 15, Monkey Bars 25, Sandbox 10. Axis 0–30 in 5s. Multiple choice tool active.
Data table showing Playground Activities values.

beats.text: In a picture graph, you count by 5s using symbols: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25.

In a bar graph, you count by 5s using the numbers written along the side of the graph on the {axis}. Look: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30. Same skip-counting, just written on the side. Both graphs count by 5s. Where do you see the 5s on the bar graph?
Where do you see the 5s on the bar graph?

params.description: Vertical picture graph titled Favorite Playground Activities. Categories: Swings (4 symbols), Slides (3 symbols), Monkey Bars (5 symbols), Sandbox (2 symbols). Scale 5: each symbol = 5. Key visible.
Vertical bar graph titled Favorite Playground Activities. Categories: Swings (20), Slides (15), Monkey Bars (25), Sandbox (10). Axis: 0 to 30 in intervals of 5. Side-by-side with picture graph.
Slides category highlights on picture graph.
Slides category highlights on bar graph.

beats.text: In a bar graph, you read the {height}, where the bar ENDS on the axis. Look at Slides. What's the {height} of the Slides bar? Where does it end?
What's the HEIGHT of the Slides bar?

params.description: Data table appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Pizza=20, Tacos=30, Salad=15, Pasta=25, Sandwiches=15.
Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Axis labeled 0-35 in 5s. Tacos bar pre-filled at 30, Sandwiches bar pre-filled at 15. Pizza, Salad, Pasta bars empty.
Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to the scale.

beats.text: Here’s new data to make a {scaled bar graph}. Students voted for their favorite lunch foods. Before you create the bars, look at the numbers on the {axis}.
These numbers count by 5s, just like you did with symbols. The axis does the skip-counting for you. What comes after 20 on this axis?
What comes after 20 on this axis?

params.description: Data table appears showing Favorite Lunch Foods values: Tacos=30, Pizza=20, Sandwiches=15, Salad=25, Pasta=10.
Vertical bar graph appears in building mode. Favorite Lunch Foods. Tacos bar pre-filled at 30, Sandwiches at 15. Pizza, Salad, Pasta bars empty. Scale: 5. Axis shows 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30.

beats.text: Pizza has 20 votes. You found 20 on the axis. Set the height of the bar for Pizza to show 20.
Set the height of the bar for Pizza to show 20.

params.description: Vertical data table titled Favorite Lunch Foods appears. Categories: Pizza (20), Tacos (30), Salad (15), Pasta (25), Sandwiches (15).
Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Pizza bar at 20, Tacos at 30, Sandwiches at 15. Salad and Pasta bars empty, ready to build.

beats.text: Finish the bar graph. Salad has 15, Pasta has 25. Set the bar heights for Salad and Pasta.
Set the bar heights for Salad and Pasta.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Pizza=20, Tacos=30, Salad=15, Pasta=25, Sandwiches=15.
Vertical picture graph appears alongside bar graph. Same Favorite Lunch Foods data. Each star symbol = 5. Pizza=4 stars, Tacos=6 stars, Salad=3 stars, Pasta=5 stars, Sandwiches=3 stars.
Data table appears showing Favorite Lunch Foods values.
Pasta bar highlights on bar graph.
Pasta column highlights on picture graph.

beats.text: Here's the same data as a picture graph. Let's check: do they match? Look at Pasta. The bar shows 25.
How many symbols should the picture graph have?

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Tacos=30, Pizza=25, Salad=15, Pasta=20, Sandwiches=15. Tacos and Salad bars highlighted.

beats.text: You already know how to compare using bar graphs when the scale is 1. Same skill works here when the axis counts by 5s. How many more students chose Tacos than Salad?
How many more students chose Tacos than Salad?

params.description: Data table appears showing numeric values only.

beats.text: So far, some parts of the graphs were already filled in for you. Now let's see if you can build a whole graph on your own.

params.description: Vertical data table appears. Books Read This Month. Liam=35, Emma=20, Noah=25, Olivia=40.
Horizontal picture graph appears. Books Read This Month. 0 categories filled. Key visible: Each ⭐ = 5 books.

beats.text: Four students tracked how many books they read this month and here is their data. This graph goes sideways, {horizontal}. Same idea as you build the graph, you just click for the {length} for the number of symbols instead of height.

elements.description: Vertical data table showing Books Read This Month: Liam 35, Emma 20, Noah 25, Olivia 40.
Horizontal picture graph in building mode. Books Read This Month. Liam row has 7 symbols placed. Emma, Noah, Olivia rows empty. Key visible: Each star = 5 books.

beats.text: Make a picture graph. Start with Liam. He read 35 books. Notice the key. Set the number of symbols for Liam.
Set the number of symbols for Liam.

params.description: Horizontal picture graph appears. Steps Taken data. Liam row filled with 7 symbols. Emma, Noah, Olivia rows empty. Key: each symbol = 5 steps.
Data table appears alongside graph showing Steps Taken values: Liam 35, Emma 20, Noah 25, Olivia 40.

beats.text: Finish the picture graph. Use the data table and complete the graph.
Set the lengths for all remaining categories.

params.description: Horizontal picture graph appears. Books Read This Month data. Liam=7 symbols (35 books), Emma=4 symbols (20 books), Noah=5 symbols (25 books), Olivia=8 symbols (40 books). Key visible: each symbol = 5 books.

beats.text: Now use your graph to find how many fewer books Emma read than Olivia.
How many fewer books did Emma read compared to Olivia?

params.description: Horizontal picture graph appears. Books Read data. Liam=35 (7 symbols), Emma=20 (4 symbols), Noah=25 (5 symbols), Olivia=40 (8 symbols). Key: Each symbol = 5 books. Scale toggle control visible with three options: Each symbol= 1, Each symbol = 2, Each symbol = 5. Currently set to scale of 5.
Data table appears alongside graph showing Books Read values: Liam=35, Emma=20, Noah=25, Olivia=40.

beats.text: You built this graph with scale of 5. But what would it look like with a different scale? Try it.
Toggle the scale to 1. What happens?

elements.description: Data table showing Books Read This Month: Liam 35, Emma 20, Noah 25, Olivia 40.
Horizontal picture graph, scale 5. Liam 7 symbols, Emma 4 symbols, Noah 5 symbols, Olivia 8 symbols. Key: Each star = 5. Scale toggle inactive.

beats.text: Olivia went from 8 symbols to 40. That's a lot of symbols. Now try scale of 2.
Toggle the scale to 2.
Fewer symbols, but still more than scale of 5. Toggle back to 5.
Toggle the scale back to 5.
Same 40 books for Olivia every time. Scale of 1: 40 symbols. Scale of 2: 20 symbols. Scale of 5: just 8.
Which scale uses the fewest symbols?
All three graphs showed the same information. The scale just changes how many symbols you need. Now let's use your scale of 5 graph to answer some questions.

params.description: Data table appears. This Week's Screen Time (minutes). Saturday=35, Sunday=30, Monday=40, Tuesday=25. 4 categories displayed numerically.
Horizontal bar graph appears. Bars extend sideways from left. Axis along bottom labeled 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45. All 4 bars empty.

beats.text: This bar graph is {horizontal}. The bars go sideways. But look at the axis along the bottom. Same skip-counting by 5s. What comes after 35 on this axis?
What comes after 35 on this axis?

params.description: Data table appears. This Week's Screen Time (minutes). Saturday=35, Sunday=30, Monday=40, Tuesday=25.
Empty horizontal bar graph template appears. Weekend Screen Time. Scale marked 0, 5, 10... up to 45.

beats.text: Now let's build the horizontal bar graph. This data shows how many minutes of screen time one person used over 4 days. Read the data, set the lengths of the bars to complete the graph.
Create the bar graph. Set all four bars.

params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. This Week's Screen Time data. Saturday=35, Sunday=30, Monday=40, Tuesday=25.
Saturday and Sunday bars highlight.

beats.text: Let's use your bar graph to answer a question. How many minutes of screen time on Saturday and Sunday in all?
How many minutes of screen time on Saturday and Sunday in all?

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Scale of 5. Shows completed work from prior section.

beats.text: You made picture graphs and bar graphs, both with scale of 5. You used skip-counting to figure out symbols and bar heights or lengths. Let's see what you know.
